name: ppa
author: dojo-community
context: Add, list, or remove Ubuntu Launchpad PPAs with add-apt-repository when a task uses ppa:user/name syntax
info: This sub-skill is the Ubuntu-specific repository shortcut for Launchpad Personal Package Archives. It wraps `add-apt-repository` for adding and removing PPAs, supports private PPAs through `--login`, and preserves the distinction between PPA convenience and broader third-party repository risk. Agents use it when vendor instructions explicitly reference `ppa:user/name`, when a Launchpad-hosted build is required, or when PPA lifecycle must be managed without manually editing source files.

sections.title: Add, remove, and list PPAs
sections.body: Use `add-apt-repository -y ppa:user/name` to add a PPA, `-r` to remove it, and `-L` to list configured repositories. Add `-n` when you do not want the command to refresh package indexes immediately.

sections.title: Private PPA authentication
sections.body: `add-apt-repository --login` authenticates to private PPAs. The command can reuse an existing Launchpad authentication token or save one through the desktop keyring or `LP_CREDENTIALS_FILE`.

sections.title: PPA risk and precedence
sections.body: A PPA is still a third-party repository with the same trust and upgrade risks as any other external source. Use [[apt/pinning]] if a PPA must stay below official Ubuntu repositories in candidate selection.

links.context: Use the general repository workflow for explicit deb822 source files
Limit PPA precedence so official packages remain preferred
Verify which source now provides the selected candidate

related.note: PPAs are a repository shortcut layered on top of the standard APT source model

scripts.name: Manage Ubuntu PPA
scripts.description: Add, remove, or list Launchpad PPAs with add-apt-repository

APT_PPA_ACTION.description: PPA action: add, remove, or list

APT_PPA.description: PPA reference, with or without the ppa: prefix

APT_ASSUME_YES.description: Set to true to pass -y to add-apt-repository

APT_NO_UPDATE.description: Set to true to pass -n and skip index refresh

LP_CREDENTIALS_FILE.description: File used to persist Launchpad credentials for private PPAs

noUpdate.description: Skip package index refresh after add or remove

depends.reason: A PPA is a specialized repository workflow within the wider APT source model

body:
# Ubuntu PPAs

PPAs are a special Ubuntu shortcut layered on top of standard APT repository configuration. Use this node when instructions explicitly reference `ppa:user/name` or the `add-apt-repository` command.

## Add and remove

```bash
sudo add-apt-repository -y ppa:deadsnakes/ppa
sudo add-apt-repository -r -y ppa:deadsnakes/ppa
sudo add-apt-repository -L
```

`add-apt-repository` can also skip the immediate cache refresh with `-n`.

## Private PPAs

```bash
export LP_CREDENTIALS_FILE="$HOME/.cache/launchpad-credentials"
sudo add-apt-repository --login ppa:team/private-archive
```

The Ubuntu manpage documents `--login` for private PPAs. Existing Launchpad credentials can be reused, or the tool can request interactive authorization.

## Operational cautions

- PPAs are still third-party repositories.
- Ubuntu's third-party repository guidance recommends pinning when you do not want an external source to outrank official packages.
- `add-apt-repository` does not add pinning automatically. See [[apt/pinning]].

If you need explicit deb822 source files and keyring control instead of the shortcut, use [[apt/repositories]].
